g.text: Layer 2 — plugin primitives (skills, the contract surface)
Layer 1 — deterministic CLIs (Python implementations behind every skill)
The loop driver does NOT live in core OR in
an impl plugin. Both lack the per-repo knowledge
required. It is checked into each consuming repo
and hand-tuned. The copier template at
starter; CI's copier-drift check excludes this
The Layer 2 contract is uniform across impl
(CI scripts, just targets) can invoke CLIs
directly. But the contract surface is the Layer 2
skill; the CLI is plumbing. Within a Claude Code
session, the Layer 3 loop invokes skills (not
CLIs) so the dispatch is namespace-aware and
Claude Code's plugin model handles it natively.
